title: Completion in bash 5 gives bash: _get_comp_words_by_ref: command not found
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Hi! I am using `Arch` with `bash 5.1.16(1)-release`, installed `bash-completion` package.

After doing `. <(podman completion bash)` pressing Tab after having typed `podman` gives this:

```bash
podman bash: _get_comp_words_by_ref: command not found
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install bash 5 and bash-completion, podman.

2. Type `podman` in a new terminal session.

3. Hit Tab.

**Describe the results you received:**
Completion does not work.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Completion works.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.1
API Version:  4.0.1
Go Version:   go1.17.8
Git Commit:   c8b9a2e3ec3630e9172499e15205c11b823c8107
Built:        Sun Mar 13 12:44:23 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
do you have the bash-completion package installed?  On Fedora it is:

```
$ rpm -q bash-completion 
bash-completion-2.11-3.fc35.noarch
```

I expect it to have the same name on Arch

Hey @giuseppe, thank you for reaching back.

I have it installed I believe:
```bash
Name            : bash-completion
Version         : 2.11-2
Description     : Programmable completion for the bash shell
Architecture    : any
URL             : https://github.com/scop/bash-completion
Licenses        : GPL2
Groups          : None
Provides        : None
Depends On      : bash
Optional Deps   : None
Required By     : None
Optional For    : bash  dotnet-host  lilv  linux-wifi-hotspot  mtr  subversion
Conflicts With  : None
Replaces        : None
Installed Size  : 851.13 KiB
Packager        : Giancarlo Razzolini <grazzolini@archlinux.org>
Build Date      : Mon 03 May 2021 01:14:45 PM CEST
Install Date    : Fri 25 Mar 2022 09:25:05 PM CET
Install Reason  : Explicitly installed
Install Script  : No
Validated By    : Signature
```

Do I need 2.11-3 for it to work?

Did you restart your shell after installing bash-completion?

I don't see this as podman issue, I would figure that this is a problem with your bash-completion package on arch.

The function is defined here https://github.com/scop/bash-completion/blob/c0f328f42eca7717a6200718ffa865bbd5e5914d/bash_completion#L392 so something is not loading it in your session.

For anyone stumbling over this, the solution for me for ubuntu was sourcing `/etc/bash_completion` in the `.bashrc`:
```sh
if [ -f /etc/bash_completion ] && ! shopt -oq posix; then
    . /etc/bash_completion
fi
```
